---
title: "Master's Thesis Seminar"
subtitle: "Seismic Hazard Mapping In Japan: effects on land and real estate prices"
author: "Anthony Rocher -- ENS Lyon"
format: revealjs
editor: visual
execute:
  echo: false
bibliography: references.bib
---

## Intuition

![Description of my research](images/pshm_2016_within_simplified_arrows.png)

## Research Question & Motivation

::: {.callout-tip title="Research Question" icon="False"}
[How costly it is to be exposed to natural disasters?]{style="color: #C0392B;"}
:::

**Why does it matter?**

**People's awareness** and behavior **about such threats**

**Investments** and maybe **inequalities** in the long run

**Intensifying disasters**: floods, hurricanes, wildfires, and droughts.

My research may apply to other type of risks

## Theoretical Part

::: callout-caution
## Still in progress

Let $p$ be the subjective probability of a earthquake to happen. $p$ depends on $i$, the information set available, and $r$, the objective attributes that make such an event more likely. Then the HPF is:

$P = P(Z, r, p(i, r))$

Expected utility becomes:

$EU = p(i, r) \cdot U^E[Z, r, Q] + (1 - p(i, r)) \cdot U^{NE}[Z, r, Q]$

Given the budget constraint $M = P(Z, r, p(i, r)) + Q$ (with $Q$ being a composite commodity), the maximization of utility is given by:

$\frac{\partial P}{\partial p}=\frac{U^{E} - U^{NE}}{p(i,r)\frac{\partial U^{E}}{\partial Q}+\left(1 - p(i,r)\right)\frac{\partial U^{NE}}{\partial Q}}$
:::

## Empirical Analysis: A Literature Review

Many papers on natural disasters and their consequences on housing prices (@brookshire_test_1985, @hidano_effect_2015, @balboni_harms_2025 among many others).

My work heavily relies on @ikefuji2022.

**My main contribution:**

-   Extending the analysis to the housing market

-   Time extension (recent years)

## Data (1)

:::::::: columns
:::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {.callout-note icon="false"}
## Risk mapping data

-   **Japan Meteorological Agency Records (JMA)**
-   all earthquakes records
-   coordinates / magnitude
-   **Japan Seismic Hazard Information Station (J-SHIS)**
-   risk hazard maps
-   uploaded every year
:::
::::

::::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {.callout-note icon="false"}
## Real estate data

-   **LIFULL HOMES**
-   rentals and sales across Japan from 2015 to 2023
:::

::: {.callout-note icon="false"}
## Other data

-   **E-STAT & OTHERS**

-   aggregated variables & macroeconomic variables
:::
:::::

Main argument of my research: **matching** **them**
::::::::

## Data (2)

::: {.column width="100%"}
![2020 Seismic Hazard Map from the J-SHIS](images/shm_2020_style.png){fig-align="left"}
:::

## Data (2)

::: {.column width="50%"}
![](images/shm_2016_final.png){fig-align="center" height="520px" style="border: 1px solid black;"}
:::

::: {.column width="3%"}
:::

::: {.column width="47%"}
Many measures of seismic risk. In this study, I choose PSHMS : <br></br>
<span style="color: grey;">Probability that a earthquake of magnitude 6 or more will happen in the next 30 years. <br></br> Let denote this probability $p$.</span>
:::

## Data (3)

### The Liffull Homes dataset:

<div style="
border: 1.5px solid #666;
padding: 14px 18px;
display: inline-block;
font-family: monospace;
font-size: 0.92rem;
line-height: 1.6;
max-width: 100%;
overflow-x: auto;
">

<table style="border-collapse: collapse;">

<tr>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px; color: orange;">pub_start_date</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px; color: orange;">pub_end_date</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px;">building_id</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px;">unit_id</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px;">status</td>
</tr>

<tr>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px; color: red;">bukken_type</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px;">all_number</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px;">empty_number</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px; color: red;">addr1_1_name</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px; color: red;">addr1_2_name</td>
</tr>

<tr>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px;">addr2_name</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px;">rosen1_name</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px;">eki1_name</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px; color: red;">walk_distance1</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px;">rosen2_name</td>
</tr>

<tr>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px;">eki2_name</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px;">walk_distance2</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px;">land_youto</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px;">land_toshi</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px; color: red;">land_area</td>
</tr>

<tr>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px; color: green;">latitude</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px; color: green;">longitude</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px;">land_right_label</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px;">house_kouzou_label</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px; color: red;">sqft</td>
</tr>

<tr>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px;">house_kaisuu</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px;">house_kaisuu_chika</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px;">kenchiku_date</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px;">flg_new</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px;">room_kaisuu</td>
</tr>

<tr>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px;">balcony_area</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px;">window_angle_label</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px;">madori_kind_all_label</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px; color: violet;">price</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px;">money_tsubo</td>
</tr>

<tr>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px;">money_kyoueki</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px;">money_reikin_amount</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px;">money_shikikin_amount</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px;">parking_money</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px;">usable_status_label</td>
</tr>

<tr>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px; color: red;">school_ele_distance</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px; color: red;">convenience_distance</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px;">super_distance</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px;">hospital_distance</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 14px;">park_distance</td>
</tr>

</table>
</div>
* Many variables, billions of observations
* Outcomes: <span style="color: violet;">price</span> & 
  <span style="color: orange;">time-on-the-market</span>


## Data (4)

##### Capturing the attractivness (city level)

<div style="
border: 1.5px solid #666;
padding: 14px 18px;
display: block;
width: 100%;
font-family: monospace;
font-size: 0.88rem;
line-height: 1.55;
overflow-x: auto;
">

<table style="
border-collapse: collapse;
table-layout: fixed;
width: 100%;
">

<tr>
<td style="padding: 2px 12px;">yearcode</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 12px;">areacode</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 12px;">area</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 12px;">comm_centers</td>
</tr>

<tr>
<td style="padding: 2px 12px;">libraries</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 12px;">lifeexp_male</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 12px; color: orange;">hospitals</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 12px;">physicians</td>
</tr>

<tr>
<td style="padding: 2px 12px;">dentists</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 12px; color: red;">new_dwellings</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 12px;">dwellings_serv</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 12px;">large_retail</td>
</tr>

<tr>
<td style="padding: 2px 12px;">kindergartens</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 12px;">elem_schools</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 12px;">elem_teachers</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 12px;">elem_students</td>
</tr>

<tr>
<td style="padding: 2px 12px;">upper_sec_sch</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 12px;">pop_total2</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 12px; color: red;">employed</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 12px;">labor_force</td>
</tr>

<tr>
<td style="padding: 2px 12px; color: orange;">skilled_workers</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 12px;">selfemp_with</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 12px;">commute_out</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 12px;">commute_in</td>
</tr>

<tr>
<td style="padding: 2px 12px; color: orange;">fin_power</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 12px;">invest_exp</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 12px;">disaster_exp</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 12px;">edu_exp_elem</td>
</tr>

<tr>
<td style="padding: 2px 12px;">edu_exp_lower</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 12px;">edu_exp_upper</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 12px;">pop_total</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 12px;">median_age</td>
</tr>

<tr>
<td style="padding: 2px 12px;">pop_o65</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 12px; color: orange;">foreigners</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 12px;">did_pop</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 12px; color: red;">inmigrants</td>
</tr>

<tr>
<td style="padding: 2px 12px; color: red;">outmigrants</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 12px;">households</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 12px;">pop_total_linear</td>
<td style="padding: 2px 12px;">lifeexp_male_linear</td>
</tr>

</table>
</div>

<div style="height: 12px;"></div>
##### and the macroeconomic conditions

Such as the interst rate, Indexes of Business Conditions (Cabinet office).

## Empirical Strategy (1)

:::: {.callout-warning title="In progress"}
::: {style="font-size: 0.8em; text-align: center;"}
$$
\begin{aligned}
\ln P_{it} = \alpha_0 &+ \sum_{j=1}^{J} \alpha_j Z_{ij} + \beta r_i + \gamma Exposed_i + \delta Post_{it} \\
&+ \theta_t (Post_{it} \times Exposed_i) + \varepsilon_{it}
\end{aligned}
$$
:::
::::

::: {style="font-size: 0.6em; line-height: 1.2;"}
-   $Z_{ij}$: vector of property and location controls
-   $r_i$: location fixed effects (prefectures) interacting with year fixed effects
-   $Exposed_i$: baseline seismic risk exposure ($p > 0.3$)
-   $Post_{it}$: $i$ is published within one month after a big earthquake (over magnitude 6)
-   $Post_{it} \times Exposed_i$: interaction capturing heterogeneous effects

Controls include aggregated data such as population, net migration, employment rate, facilities (e.g. number of hospitals, shcools, a municipality financial index), and some macro variables (GDP, business index)
:::

## Now

Trying to figure out huge data files

Generating descriptive statistics

Working on the empirical equation:

* how long the effects last after a big earthquake
* should I opt for a continuous treatment?

## Questions (1)

Quelles sont les variables à garder / à jeter ?

::: {style="max-height: 450px; overflow-y: auto; font-size: 0.72em;"}
+------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Catégorie                          | Types de biens                                                    |
+:===================================+:==================================================================+
| Terrains & droits fonciers         | Land for Sale\                                                    |
|                                    | Land\                                                             |
|                                    | Transfer of Leasehold Rights\                                     |
|                                    | Transfer of Underlying Land Rights                                |
+------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Résidentiel — maisons              | Newly Built Detached House\                                       |
|                                    | Used Detached House\                                              |
|                                    | Detached house\                                                   |
|                                    | Newly Built Terrace House\                                        |
|                                    | Used Terrace House\                                               |
|                                    | Terrace house\                                                    |
|                                    | Newly Built Townhouse\                                            |
|                                    | Used Townhouse\                                                   |
|                                    | Townhouse\                                                        |
|                                    | Vacation home\                                                    |
|                                    | Company housing                                                   |
+------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Résidentiel — appartements         | Newly Built Apartment\                                            |
|                                    | Used Apartment\                                                   |
|                                    | Apartment\                                                        |
|                                    | Condominium                                                       |
+------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Résidentiel — logements collectifs | Newly Built Public Housing\                                       |
|                                    | Used Public Housing\                                              |
|                                    | Room rental\                                                      |
|                                    | Dormitory\                                                        |
|                                    | Dormitory/Boarding house                                          |
+------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Commercial & bureaux               | Shop\                                                             |
|                                    | Store\                                                            |
|                                    | Shop with Residence\                                              |
|                                    | Residence with Shop\                                              |
|                                    | Shop/Office\                                                      |
|                                    | Office\                                                           |
|                                    | Shop (entire building)\                                           |
|                                    | Shop (part of building)\                                          |
|                                    | Residential property with commercial space (part of the building) |
+------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Industriel & logistique            | Factory\                                                          |
|                                    | Warehouse                                                         |
+------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Immeubles & bâtiments              | Building                                                          |
+------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Hébergement & tourisme             | Inn\                                                              |
|                                    | Hotel\                                                            |
|                                    | Resort Apartment\                                                 |
|                                    | Resort Mansion                                                    |
+------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Stationnement                      | Parking lot                                                       |
+------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Autres                             | Other                                                             |
+------------------------------------+-------------------------------------------------------------------+
:::

## References
